Our friends at Motherduck are hosting the upcoming event at their office in Amsterdam at the Eastern Docklands

17:30 – 🍕 Welcome

18:00 – 🎤 dbt in Snowflake - First experiences and comparisons to dbt Cloud
Chris Verweij (Data Engineer) @ Nimbus Intelligence

18:30 – 🎤 DuckLake + dbt: Time traveling with SQL, metadata, and a little AI
Leonardo Vida (Customer Engineer) @ MotherDuck

19:00 – 🎤 The state of Freelance Data Jobs in the fall of 2025, using dashboarding-as-code
Lasse Benninga (Data Freelancer) @ LaBenni Consulting

19:30 –🥤 Drinks & Snacks

---

About the speakers:

🎤 Chris Verweij (Nimbus Intelligence)
Chris is a data and -analytics engineer consulting on all matters Snowflake and dbt. Specializing in custom ingestion pipelines, DataOps CI/CD and data application development, she enjoys pushing platforms and products to their maximum potential. Her current consultancy work is focused in the healthcare sector, guiding data from ingestion to dashboarding.

🎤 Leonardo Vida (MotherDuck)
Leonardo Vida helps data teams of all sizes adopt DuckDB-powered analytics across cloud and local stacks. Formerly a senior data engineer and consultant, he designed data platforms across sectors from FMCG to insurance, and is interested in database-centric AI workloads, particularly building analytics agents with DuckDB.

🎤 Lasse Benninga (LaBenni Consulting)
*I’m Lasse Benninga, a freelance data/analytics engineer with 7+ years of experience in the data field. Having worked as a consultant for the past 5 years, I’ve now transitioned into freelancing and help clients with a variety of challenges from scanning their current data stack to helping implement data warehouses and software solutions. I also offer a scan for SMB to help them in their next steps to increase data maturity and AI-readiness: https://data-apk.nl*

---

About the talks:

dbt in Snowflake - First experiences and comparisons to dbt Cloud
Taking a first look at running dbt projects natively in Snowflake Workspaces, the advantages and disadvantages compared to dbt Cloud, and whether moving right now fits your usecase. With hands-on demonstration and some extra tips on connecting Snowflake to your remote git dbt repository.

The state of Freelance Data Jobs in the fall of 2025, using dashboarding-as-code
Dashboarding-as-code, the practice of defining dashboards using code instead of solely in the UI, has become more popular in recent years. In this talk I will show the benefits of defining charts and visuals using code, while simultaneously diving into the Dutch Freelancing market for “Data jobs” over the past couple of months. We will take a look at the noticeable effects of the Dutch Tax Authority rules for false-employment/schijnzelfstandigheid in the data field, and which job titles and technologies are hot nowadays while showing the power of code for quick iterations.

DuckLake + dbt: Time traveling with SQL, metadata, and a little AI
We’ll take a hands-on look at using DuckLake behind dbt: how inserts land, how to time travel your data, and how to see what changed between runs. I’ll show some handy dbt macros plus a few tricks for skipping a heavy metrics layer and leaning on AI to suggest columns and comments instead.

----

---
About the talks

Talk: Entity-First Driven Design for Event Data Modeling

Behavioral data is rich, granular, and highly predictive for understanding how customers make decisions. However, it can also be messy and complex, becoming increasingly fragmented over time in downstream modeling. In this talk, we’ll go back to the source of data creation and explore why an Entity-first approach to event data design & modeling can not only transform raw event data into reliable data models and improve analytics quality, but also enable future operational use cases.

Jon Su is a Solutions Architect at Snowplow, collaborating closely with and guiding businesses to design and build out their best-in-class customer data infrastructure on Snowplow. With experience working with customers across retail, fintech, media, and other industries, Jon helps organizations unlock operational value from their behavioral data and turn data into outcomes. Prior to joining Snowplow, Jon worked as the EU Regional Data Analytics Lead for a boutique Google Cloud consultancy where he supported customers with Martech and Cloud technical implementations and roadmap strategies.

Daniel Herrera (Analytics Engineer & Developer Advocate**)** @ Teradata

19:00 –🥤 Drinks & Snacks

---
About the talks

🎤 Lights, dbt, Action: Making Analytics Engineering Visible (and Fast)

In 2022, our team of three data analysts at Floryn implemented dbt to build a more scalable and structured analytics workflow. At the time, most of our business logic was embedded in LookML within Looker, and dbt was more of a “nice to have” than a core component of our workflow. That changed last year when we migrated to a new BI tool, forcing us to extract all our LookML-based transformations into dbt.

This transition made us realize how much of our logic had been siloed within Looker, and it became the catalyst for fully centralizing our data models in dbt. By making dbt the foundation of our data analytics products, we standardized data transformation, improved data quality, and created a more scalable approach to managing our data.

Beyond improving our data models, dbt has enabled us to develop entirely new analytics products that wouldn’t have been possible before. With dbt as our single source of truth, our analytics engineers can now build cleaner, more reliable models while ensuring consistency across all reporting and analysis. We’ve leveraged dbt to develop metric trees that provide deeper insights into business performance as well as a data-driven warning system. By making dbt central to our analytics strategy, we’ve enhanced trust in our data and unlocked new opportunities for delivering meaningful insights.

In this talk, we'll share our journey from LookML-dependent modeling to a fully dbt-driven analytics framework, the challenges we faced, and the lessons we learned along the way. Whether you're considering dbt for your organization or looking to scale your analytics capabilities, our story highlights the power of a well-structured, centralized data strategy

🎤 Data analyst AI Agent powered by dbt Metadata

Generative AI adjacent concepts terms like "Agentic AI" or "vibe coding," are frequently used or misused as marketing hooks rather than as practical frameworks for understanding the technical reality of generative AI.

In this talk, we aim to cut through the noise by building a data analyst AI agent completely from scratch. We will not rely on any libraries or frameworks. Instead, we will focus on what it actually takes to create an agent that can generate insights from data.

One of the biggest challenges when working with large language models for sql query generation is providing the right context. Helping the model understand the structure and meaning of your data — including databases, tables, and columns and what they contain — is often the hardest part. However, if you are using a dbt, you already have access to rich metadata that can be passed to the model. This gives it the necessary understanding of the available data structures to generate accurate queries.

In this session, we will walk through how to use that metadata effectively and how to connect an LLM to your database. By the end, you’ll have a clear understanding of what it takes to build a functioning data analyst agent from the ground up.

---

Location:
Nimbus Intelligence
Magriet Toren
13th floor

---

A strategic approach to testing with Data Products in dbt
With data teams' growing ambition to build business automation, AI systems, or customer-facing products, we must shift our mindset about data quality. Mechanically applied testing will not be enough; we need a more robust strategy similar to software engineering.In this talk, I outline a new approach to data testing and observability anchored in the ‘Data Products’ concept and walk through the practical implementation of a production-grade analytics system with dbt as the backbone.The learnings will be applicable for data practitioners using dbt no matter if they're just getting started or work in a large enterprise.

Lessons from setting up 100+ teams’ data stacks
A distillation of some of the lessons I’ve learned from helping more than 100 teams build or migrate their data stacks to be usable by their organisations.

---
About the speakers

Mikkel is the co-founder of SYNQ, a data observability platform. He previously led data teams at Monzo and Google and writes about all-things-data on https://mikkeldengsoe.substack.com/

Hamzah is the co-founder & CEO of Lightdash, a BI platform built natively for dbt. A software engineer who previously setup and scaled a data consultancy working with folks like the National Grid and Department of Education.

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

---

About the speakers:

🎤 Sam Debruyn (dataroots)
Sam Debruyn is a Cloud Data Solution Architect with a passion for the modern data stack. In his day-to-day, he designs and implements data platforms that support customers in harvesting the full potential of their data. As a true believer in open-source, he is a top contributor to dbt and several adapters and packages. Sam is also a dbt Community Award winner, organizer of the Belgium dbt Meetups, and a frequent speaker at data meetups and conferences.

🎤 Ruben Huidekoper (Xebia) & Bart Manintveld (The Future Group)
Ruben Huidekoper and Bart Manintveld are using DBT to develop a centralized data platform at Van Oord, one of world's leading dredging and marine contracting companies.

---

About the talks:

dbt at Van Oord: A case study on how we log runs and handle our HR data
In this presentation, they will give an overview of the platform architecture and DBT's role in it, and discuss the specific challenges encountered when doing analytics engineering at Van Oord.
Furthermore, they will present two analytics engineering case studies: how we handle DBT run logs, and SQL patterns which are commonly encountered when working with HR data. (edited)

From Fabric to Fantastic: How dbt Makes Your Lakehouses and Warehouses Shine
Unlock the full potential of your Microsoft Fabric Data Warehouse or Lakehouse. In this session, analytics engineers can learn from the top contributor to dbt-fabric what Microsoft Fabric is all about and how dbt is the perfect tool to build data transformations on Microsoft Fabric. We’ll cover the basics of Microsoft Fabric, how to connect dbt Cloud with Microsoft Fabric, and how to build our first Hello Fabric project.

Databricks ❤️ Why the lakehouse is the best place to run dbt.
Learn why the lakehouse is the best to run dbt projects. We will cover how to get started, recent improvements and awesome features on our roadmap.

Count canvas - The new number one tool in data workflows in Otrium

We will cover practical examples that show why it has become day-to-day tool for all Otrium's data analysts, scientists and engineers.

His versatile background positions him as a powerful force in driving meaningful change in the healthcare and technology sectors.

Jeroen Visser

Ricardo Angel Granados López

Details

Get ready for the 3rd dbt Meetup in the Netherlands!

This time brought to you by https://vandebron.nl/Vandebron!

Schedule:

17:30 – 🍕 Welcome.

18:00 – 🎤 Utilizing Temporal Tables in dbt Incremental Models

18:30 – 🎤 Reigniting Data Modeling: Sensible Strategies for the 2020s and Beyond

19:00 – 🎤 Building on top of dbt

19:30 –🥤 Drinks

---

🎤 Utilizing Temporal Tables in dbt Incremental Models

Daniel Herrera | Principal Developer Advocate at Teradata

This session provides an in-depth examination of the integration of temporal tables within a dbt project. We'll elucidate how temporal tables serve as an efficient method for tracking newly added or updated records, thereby enhancing the efficiency of incremental models. Additionally, we'll highlight alternative strategies, including dbt snapshots, that can achieve comparable outcomes.

Discussion Points:

Review of Incremental Models: Understanding incremental models and their runs.

Insights into Temporal Tables: Dive into the functionalities of the TRANSACTIONTIME PERIOD fields and comprehend their significance in monitoring record additions and updates.

Harnessing Temporal Data in DBT: An extensive analysis on how dbt models can leverage temporal markers to filter and ingest only the newly added or updated data.

DBT Snapshots: An exploration of snapshots as an alternative approach to temporal tables.

---

🎤 Reigniting Data Modeling: Sensible Strategies for the 2020s and Beyond

Niko Korvenlaita | CTO at reconfigured

This talk covers theoretical and practical learnings from Niko's journey to productize data modeling best practices into performant dbt code, across major data warehouses. In this talk Niko will cover long forgotten data modeling best practices, as well as design you can apply to your own dbt projects. Be prepared: This is not going to be jaffle shop but instead we’ll dive to the deep end with incrementality, temporality and event ledgers. All whilst not blowing up your compute costs. Niko will also share practical learnings on how to build agile data processes in DWH centric setup.

---

🎤 Building on top of dbt

Mats Stijlaart | Analytics Engineer at Vandebron

Pieter Custers | Tech Lead Data Analytics at Vandebron

dbt takes a central position in the data stack, but is never the start or the end of any data pipeline. In this talk Vandebron will show how they use Dagster — a data orchestration tool — and Lightdash — a self-service BI-tool — to seamlessly integrate Dbt in their stack.

Dagster and Dbt have in common that they recognize data assets as the first class citizens of a data pipeline. This common characteristic makes that they integrate very well with each other. The benefit of using Dagster is that its orchestration capabilities go beyond the data warehouse. This makes it possible to integrate dbt in a larger context, from ingestion to consumption of the data. Located at the consumption end, Lightdash is designed specifically to operate on top of dbt, and thereby lets you leverage the full potential of your data marts.

---

Schedule:

18:00 – 🎤 CI for dbt: Beyond the basics!
18:30 – 🎤 Programmable dbt models: how to automate GDPR deletion requests
19:00 – 🎤 Data alerts are hard
19:30 –🥤 Drinks

---

🎤 CI for dbt: Beyond the basics!
Pádraic Slattery | Analytics Engineer at Xebia Data

As organizations continue to generate massive amounts of data, the challenges of scaling data management have become increasingly complex. To remain competitive, data teams must find ways to scale their operations and deliver high-quality data quickly. Continuous Integration (CI) has emerged as a best practice that is gaining popularity with dbt users. By automatically building and testing code changes before deployment, CI helps data teams streamline their development workflows, reduce errors, and deliver high-quality data.

In this talk, we will explore the benefits of using CI with dbt and how it can help data teams scale their operations. We will discuss how to set up a CI pipeline with dbt and cover best practices for testing data transformations. We will also share real-world examples that can be applied to any CI setup including; building models in a dedicated schema, reducing CI data scans using jinja filters and running pytests against the JSON artifacts produced in the CI run. We have seen great success with these CI configurations at our clients as they allow organisations to enforce their conventions and standards automatically, therefore allowing developers to concentrate on delivering new features rather than spending large quantities of time reviewing pull requests.

Attendees will leave this talk with a clear understanding of how CI with dbt can help data teams scale their development and deliver high-quality data faster. They will also gain practical knowledge of how to set up a CI pipeline with dbt including some tests that will benefit any dbt codebase.

---

🎤 Programmable dbt models: how to automate GDPR deletion requests
Maxime Casara & Matthieu Caneill | Data Engineers at Picnic

dbt allows data engineers to reason about models as first-class citizens of the ecosystem. Most dbt entities like models, sources, exposures, tests and seeds are created and configured declaratively in YAML. This opens the door for programmable dbt models. Materializations and other macros can exploit model metadata and derive side-effects from them. In this talk, we will go over the concept of programmable dbt models, how we use it at Picnic, and illustrate it with the example of GDPR deletions.

---

🎤 Data alerts are hard
Mikkel Dengsøe | Co-founder of Synq

Managing the alerting workflow is no easy task as you scale. On one side you want to know about issues as they happen but at the same time you don’t want to end up flooded with data alerts that don’t get actioned.

When data teams don’t find the right balance and end up having too many alerts that are unaddressed it can lead to negative consequences such as:

Important issues get overlooked
Triaging and monitoring alerts become a time sink
The data team loses confidence in the impact of writing tests

In this talk I’ll dive into what a better alerting workflow can look like and some steps you can take to bring this into your alerts. The talk will cover:

Different type of metadata you can surface in alerts such as ownership, importance and root cause analysis that can help you prioritise, resolve and debug issues
Ways in which you can use alerts to bring relevant information to people outside the data team such as upstream engineering or operations teams
Specific steps you can take to get this metadata and build it into your workflow

---

🎤 Scaling data teams — three pitfalls and how to avoid them
Thomas in't Veld | CEO at Tasman Analytics

Scaling data teams from zero is hard: there are no easy shortcuts, and it is hard to find clear examples to learn from! In this talk we will share our secrets on rapidly deploying analytics for our clients. As a business, we focus on setting up data infrastructure, data models and reporting, and do it as fast and as well as we can — but we also want to make sure that what we built is scaleable, and still relevant long after we leave.

The modern data stack is a phenomenally powerful ecosystem for this. It allows us to have modular data pipelines, growth engines, reproducible analysis and reverse ETL — and beyond that, tools like dbt also institutionalise documentation, integrate engineering processes into data model delivery. But what are the best approaches? Does the ubiquity of these great tools mean that we need to think about data modelling in a different way now? How can we be sure that the pipeline we can quickly build right now is still relevant in a few months time, let alone five years? We will take you through three common mistakes data teams make, and tell you what — at least in our experience — you can do to avoid them!

🎤 Polaroids of your data: capturing changes in tables using dbt snapshots
Anya Prosvetova | Analytics Engineer at Cosuno

Do you need to know how your data evolves over time? In this talk Anya will give a brief introduction to dbt snapshots, a feature that enables you to keep track of when and how values in your data changed. She will also show how to use this feature through practical examples.

🎤 Why the lakehouse is the best place for your dbt project
Bilal Aslam | Sr. Director of Product Management at Databricks

In this talk, we go from the basics of how to develop your first debt project on the Databricks lakehouse, all the way to scaling it with best practices, including with Unity Catalog.
